name: Tempeh Chili
recipeIngredient: 28 oz Canned tomatoes, peeled and chopped with juice
15 oz Canned kidney beans, drained and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, marinate the Tempeh with soy sauce, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, mustard powder, cumin, chili powder, salt, and black pepper for 1 hour, turning after 30 minutes.
2. Grate the Tempeh and set aside with the marinade.
3. In a large pan, heat the remaining ingredients (soy or sesame oil, olive oil, green pepper, onion) over medium heat until hot.
4. Add the grated Tempeh (with marinade) to the pan and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
5. Stir in the chopped tomatoes, canned tomatoes with juice, kidney beans, and additional soy sauce or tamari.
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
7. Serve hot, topped with grated cheddar cheese (optional).
8. Tempeh is an Indonesian cholesterol-free staple made from tender cooked, cultured organic soybeans. It can be fried, broiled, baked, or steamed and used in various recipes as a meat substitute or served on pizza, sandwiches, or in salads.
